records.ety: [F. fomenter, fr. L. fomentare, fr. fomentum (for fovimentum) a warm application or lotion, fr. fovere to warm or keep warm; perh. akin to Gr. <?/ to roast, and E. bake.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Fomented; p. pr. & vb. n. Fomenting.]

defs: To apply a warm lotion to; to bathe with a cloth or sponge wet with warm water or medicated liquid.
To cherish with heat; to foster.
quotes: Which these soft fires . . . foment and warm.

defs: To nurse to life or activity; to cherish and promote by excitements; to encourage; to abet; to instigate; -- used often in a bad sense; as, to foment ill humors.
quotes: But quench the choler you foment in vain.
Exciting and fomenting a religious rebellion.

defs: State of excitation; -- perh. confused with ferment.
quotes: He came in no conciliatory mood, and the foment was kept up.
